hook.text: A team ran the same failed experiment three times in two years. Nobody documented the first result. They wasted six months.

buildup.text: Experiment documentation creates institutional memory: what was tested, why, the result, and the learnings.

discovery.text: A good experiment doc includes: hypothesis, metrics, sample size, duration, results, statistical significance, and key takeaways.

twist.text: Failed experiments are the most valuable to document.

climax.text: Netflix maintains a searchable experiment library. Any team can check if their idea has been tested before.

punchline.text: Document every experiment. Your future self will thank you.

quiz.question: Why is documenting failed experiments especially valuable?
options: It prevents repeating mistakes and reveals user preferences that inform future decisions
Only successful experiments contain useful learnings
